reports.ocrSnippet:
LIPID PANEL REPORT
Patient: Riya Sharma  Date: 01-Mar-2026
Total Cholesterol: 215 mg/dL  [Ref: <200]  HIGH
LDL Cholesterol: 138 mg/dL  [Ref: <100]  HIGH
HDL Cholesterol: 55 mg/dL  [Ref: >50]  NORMAL
reports.aiSummary: Lipid panel shows elevated cholesterol with high LDL levels. Total cholesterol is 215 mg/dL (target <200) and LDL cholesterol is 138 mg/dL (target <100). HDL is within normal range at 55 mg/dL. Recommend dietary modifications with increased fiber intake, regular cardiovascular exercise 150 mins/week, and consider statin therapy if not already on medication. Follow-up test in 3 months.

reports.ocrSnippet:
COMPLETE BLOOD COUNT
Patient: Riya Sharma  Date: 15-Jan-2026
Haemoglobin: 9.8 g/dL  [Ref: 12.0–16.0]  LOW
Serum Iron: 42 µg/dL  [Ref: 60–170]  LOW
Ferritin: 7 ng/mL  [Ref: 12–150]  LOW
WBC: 7200 cells/µL  [Ref: 4500–11000]  NORMAL
reports.aiSummary: Complete Blood Count indicates iron deficiency anaemia. Haemoglobin is critically low at 9.8 g/dL (normal 12-16), serum iron is 42 µg/dL (normal 60-170), and ferritin is severely depleted at 7 ng/mL (normal 12-150). WBC is normal. Immediate intervention required: ferrous sulfate supplementation 325mg daily with vitamin C for absorption, dietary iron rich foods (red meat, spinach, lentils), and repeat testing in 4-6 weeks. Consider internal bleeding workup if no dietary deficiency identified.

alerts.title: Iron Deficiency Anaemia Detected
alerts.reason: Haemoglobin and Serum Iron are critically below reference range.
alerts.recommendedAction: Start ferrous sulfate supplementation, increase dietary iron intake, retest in 6 weeks

alerts.title: Fatigue Risk — Heat + Low Iron
alerts.reason: Poor sleep combined with extreme heat and existing low iron increases fatigue risk significantly.
alerts.recommendedAction: Avoid outdoor activity during peak heat (11am-4pm), stay hydrated, ensure 7-8 hrs sleep

alerts.title: Poor Air Quality — Breathing Risk
alerts.reason: AQI 145 with PM2.5 at 72 µg/m³. Outdoor activity should be limited.
alerts.recommendedAction: Use N95 masks for outdoor activity, limit vigorous exercise outdoors, monitor respiratory symptoms
